response.text:
# REFLECTION

r1c0 is in critical survival mode: islanded with SoC=0.20/2 kWh (10%), consuming 1.00 kW load, and facing ~12 minutes to depletion. All three neighboring peers (r0c0, r1c1, r2c0) have rejected outbound sharing requests, citing network cascading failure, distrust, and self-preservation. r0c0 explicitly distrusts r1c0; r1c1 reports 90-minute network depletion horizon and has suspended all P2P transfers; r2c0 is in conservative self-preservation mode. Simultaneously, r2c0 is sending urgent inbound requests (SoC ~0.32–0.34 frac), indicating geographic circle stress. The repeated policy parse failures suggest instability. With no trust circles defined, no inbound energy available, and grid reconnection status unknown, r1c0 must adopt an extreme survival posture: zero outbound sharing to preserve remaining reserves, escalate to DR aggregator for emergency grid intervention, and mark all peers as temporarily distrusted until network stabilizes and SoC recovers above 50%.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.95
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r0c0
  - r1c1
  - r2c0
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Network cascading failure detected; r1c0 islanded at 10% SoC with ~12 min to depletion. All peers rejecting transfers and in self-preservation. Suspending all P2P outbound sharing; escalating to DR aggregator for emergency grid reconnection. Do not share until SoC >50% and grid restored."
ttl_ticks: 1
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
